TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: izua za sitsuayan a ti Saulivan a mamazangiɫan, pakasengseng ta sikataqaɫan ta quma.
S.PHON: izua za siʦuajan a ti sauɭivan a mamazaŋiʎan, pakasəŋsəŋ ta sikataqaʎan ta quma.
S.FORM: izua za sicuayan a ti Saulivan a mamazangiljan, pakasengseng ta sikataqaljan ta quma.
S.PHON: izua za siʦuajan a ti sauɭivan a mamazaŋiʎan, pakasəŋsəŋ ta sikataqaʎan ta quma.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a chief called Saulivan. He made his fellow-villagers work in his fields.

S.FORM: "izua vava," aya ventsa; mavan a sika sengseng ta quma nimadju.
S.PHON: "izua vava," aja vənʦa; mavan a sika səŋsəŋ ta quma nimaɉu.
S.FORM: "izua vava," aya venca; mavan a sika sengseng ta quma nimadju.
S.PHON: "izua vava," aja vənʦa; mavan a sika səŋsəŋ ta quma nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: He lied and said: “There’s beer for you.” That’s why they worked in his fields.

S.FORM: "keɫu, tekelu," aya.
S.PHON: "kəʎu, təkəɭu," aja.
S.FORM: "kelju, tekelu," aya.
S.PHON: "kəʎu, təkəɭu," aja.
S.TRANSL: “Come and get a drink,” he said.

S.FORM: ka sutsaqevan a djilung, manu neka nu vava, a pangats i taladj.
S.PHON: ka suʦaqəvan a ɉiɭuŋ, manu nəka nu vava, a paŋaʦ i taɭaɉ.
S.FORM: ka sucaqevan a djilung, manu neka nu vava, a pangac i taladj.
S.PHON: ka suʦaqəvan a ɉiɭuŋ, manu nəka nu vava, a paŋaʦ i taɭaɉ.
S.TRANSL: When the lid was taken off the pot, there was no beer; there were hornets inside.

S.FORM: mavilad a sikataqaɫan.
S.PHON: maviɭad a sikataqaʎan.
S.FORM: mavilad a sikataqaljan.
S.PHON: maviɭad a sikataqaʎan.

S.FORM: izua qatsiɫay a kinelay i paɫing.
S.PHON: izua qaʦiʎaj a kinəɭaj i paʎiŋ.
S.FORM: izua qaciljay a kinelay i paljing.
S.PHON: izua qaʦiʎaj a kinəɭaj i paʎiŋ.
S.TRANSL: There was a slate slab hung in the doorway.

S.FORM: ka mavilad setsugtsug; madudu a sikataqaɫan.
S.PHON: ka maviɭad səʦugʦug; madudu a sikataqaʎan.
S.FORM: ka mavilad secugcug; madudu a sikataqaljan.
S.PHON: ka maviɭad səʦugʦug; madudu a sikataqaʎan.
S.TRANSL: When they ran away, they bumped into it. The villagers were angry.

S.FORM: "keɫu, kisudjui," aya a sikataqaɫan tjai Saulivan, "a sema Padain," aya pasukangalu.
S.PHON: "kəʎu, kisuɉui," aja a sikataqaʎan cai sauɭivan, "a səma padain," aja pasukaŋaɭu.
S.FORM: "kelju, kisudjui," aya a sikataqaljan tjai Saulivan, "a sema Padain," aya pasukangalu.
S.PHON: "kəʎu, kisuɉui," aja a sikataqaʎan ʦai sauɭivan, "a səma padain," aja pasukaŋaɭu.
S.TRANSL: “Come on, let’s go to Padain to visit the girls,” the villagers lied to Saulivan.

S.FORM: pitaladjen timadju ta tjaɫev, sa qalui sa vaikan a kemelu pasa zua i ta tjaɫitiw; matsay timadju.
S.PHON: pitaɭaɉən timaɉu ta caʎəv, sa qaɭui sa vaikan a kəməɭu pasa zua i ta caʎitiw; maʦaj timaɉu.
S.FORM: pitaladjen timadju ta tjaljev, sa qalui sa vaikan a kemelu pasa zua i ta tjaljitiw; macay timadju.
S.PHON: pitaɭaɉən timaɉu ta ʦaʎəv, sa qaɭui sa vaikan a kəməɭu pasa zua i ta ʦaʎitiw; maʦaj timaɉu.
S.TRANSL: They put him in a box, and went off carrying it, and tipped him down a waterfall. He died

S.FORM: mekasiw timadju.
S.PHON: məkasiw timaɉu.
S.FORM: mekasiw timadju.
S.PHON: məkasiw timaɉu.

S.FORM: venangaɫ ta kaɫipa.
S.PHON: vənaŋaʎ ta kaʎipa.
S.FORM: venangalj ta kaljipa.
S.PHON: vənaŋaʎ ta kaʎipa.

S.FORM: ka maipuq a kasiw, malap a katsalisian ta kaɫipa.
S.PHON: ka maipuq a kasiw, maɭap a kaʦaɭisian ta kaʎipa.
S.FORM: ka maipuq a kasiw, malap a kacalisian ta kaljipa.
S.PHON: ka maipuq a kasiw, maɭap a kaʦaɭisian ta kaʎipa.
S.TRANSL: When the tree fell, mountain people collected the shells.

S.FORM: mavan a sika izua na kaɫipa tutsu.
S.PHON: mavan a sika izua na kaʎipa tuʦu.
S.FORM: mavan a sika izua na kaljipa tucu.
S.PHON: mavan a sika izua na kaʎipa tuʦu.
S.TRANSL: That’s why there are kaɫipa shells to this day.
